title: Digest header checked with constant-time comparison
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:863-878

FINDING: The SHA-256 body digest comparison uses subtle::ConstantTimeEq rather than a plain == comparison. While a timing attack on a hash comparison is extremely difficult to exploit (attacker would need sub-nanosecond timing resolution over millions of requests), using ct_eq here is the principled choice and signals to future readers that security properties of this comparison have been considered. It also future-proofs the code against environments with less stable timing.

Should be preserved; a reviewer might propose replacing with == 'since it's a hash not the secret itself', but constant-time comparison of authentication-adjacent values is a good hygiene habit worth maintaining.
close_reason: Acknowledged: digest ct_eq design is correct
